title: Smoking Hot British Duck Steamed Pudding
description: A classic British dish featuring duck fat, smoked to perfection, served as a moist and flavorful dessert. Inspired by traditional steamed puddings, this recipe combines the best of monsoon British flavors.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, breadcrumbs, baking powder, smoked salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted duck fat, granulated sugar, eggs, and whole milk.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Add the chopped parsley and smoked paprika, and mix until just combined.
Transfer the batter to a 1-pint steamer basket, and steam for 1 1/2 hours, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from heat, and let cool for 10 minutes.
Serve warm, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
For an added touch, garnish with additional chopped parsley and a sprinkle of smoked paprika.
Let the pudding sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before serving, allowing it to set.
This pudding is best served within a day of making, as it will retain its moisture and flavor.
You can also store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, warming it before serving.
